steps: \(f(t) = 55t - 2t^2\) is a downward-opening parabola with \(a = -2\), \(b = 55\). Its maximum occurs at \(t^* = -\frac{b}{2a} = \frac{55}{4}\).
Since \(g(t) = f(t) + 3\), the maximum of \(g\) is \(f(t^*) + 3 = 3 + 2\left(\frac{55}{4}\right)^2\).
choiceEliminations:
• Choice A — '\(3 + \left(\frac{55}{2}\right)^2\)': This equals \(3 + \frac{55^2}{4}\), which is twice the correct vertex value of \(\frac{55^2}{8}\). ✗
  • Choice B — '\(3 + 2\left(\frac{55}{4}\right)^2\)': Equals \(3 + \frac{55^2}{8}\), which matches the computed maximum exactly. ✓
  • Choice C — '\(3 - 2\left(\frac{55}{4}\right)^2\)': Subtracts the vertex value instead of adding it; gives a result well below zero. ✗
  • Choice D — '\(3 - \left(\frac{55}{2}\right)^2\)': Subtracts the wrong (larger) quantity; gives a large negative number. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
